## [00:00 - 03:04]

Alright guys, so I want to show you guys how to set up your reporting columns inside of your ad account so that every single time that you log in to Ads Manager you can quickly assess how your funnel is performing and pull up the stats that are actually relevant to you. If you are using the default sort of settings in the reporting this is kind of what you're gonna see right so Facebook will show you you know if you're running a traffic campaign it'll show you link clicks, post engagement, if you're running a conversion based campaign it will show you the event. Now this doesn't always show you the actual event that you're trying to track if you're using custom conversions and also it doesn't show you all the other steps in your funnel so it can be very easy to get misled into thinking well this campaign isn't working because my purchases are coming in at you know four times the price I'm comfortable to pay when you're not actually looking at every step along your funnel to quickly isolate what the actual bottleneck is and then addressing that instead of just rolling out new audiences or new campaigns or touching and tweaking things that really shouldn't have been touched to begin with. So the first thing is to really map out those metrics along your funnel so this is the e-commerce funnel number four here and you'll see here this is a template so just be aware of that but you'll see here it's got some basic metrics around certain figures that we're looking for okay so at the beginning we're looking at click through rates and CPC we also want to measure bounce rates so we'll use landing page views there to track that landing page conversions so this means there would be a standard event behind the lead magnet that we can track for we want to track things like conversion rates so purchases and then also abandon cart rates so those are the important metrics that we want to track for so the first thing we're going to do is go into the ad account and we'll input all of these into a customized column and I'm also going to get you guys to add a few more things in there as well that will just help you assess your funnels so what you're going to do is click on columns performance customized columns and we're going to leave reach and impressions we're going to remove cost per result you can leave your budget and amount spent that's obviously important I'm going to really release and release remove those relevant score and frequency are good and unique clicks we definitely want in there okay so if we go back in here the first metric we want to add is our click-through rate now we're looking for we're looking for link clicks here so this is the one that we want here not

## [03:04 - 06:05]

all okay because that's going to give you a skewed result the second thing here is we are measuring our bounce rate so we're basically trying to make sure that our traffic is not a problem or our website is not a problem and so how you would measure bounce rate is essentially compare how many unique clicks you've sent versus how many people loaded the page now Facebook by default will track what we call landing page views you don't have to install this on your page you don't have to do anything it's it's automatically tracked through Facebook so we're going to click that here and essentially if we divide the number of landing page views by the number of unique link clicks that will give us our bounce rate we're looking at landing page conversions because we've got a lead magnet here so we're looking at a landing page conversion here and we want to basically track the event that is going to be on the thank you page after people opt in so that we can track that so let's just say that that was complete registration and we'll go website complete cost per completed registration so we can also assess that so you may want to add that in your funnel you may want to say like max $15 per regio so you can see if this is a potential bottleneck in your funnel the second thing that the last thing not last thing the next thing that we want to track is view content here so how many people have viewed content now this metric is a little bit skewed and it's kind of a nice to have but we don't really use this one to assess bottlenecks so much with the exception that if you have a lot of landing page views but no content views then you know there's a problem people are not actually scrolling through your website they're not viewing products they're not they're not shopping essentially right because view content especially on Shopify or WooCommerce gets triggered when people are looking at products so we're going to look at that and then the next thing is our purchase we're going to do cost per purchase as well and then our abandon cart rate which means we need to track or add to cart rate and to calculate the abandon cart rate all you do is divide the number of add to carts you divide the number of purchases by the number of add to carts and then we're going to do cost per add to cart okay some other metrics that we want in here as well is our positive and negative feedback it's just always good to be aware of what's happening here you will probably find your ad account will have negative feedback but as long as positive feedback is from medium to high then you're okay if you have low positive feedback and high negative feedback you run the risk of having that ad account shut down so just be aware of

## [06:05 - 08:23]

that another important metric that we want in here is cost per clicks so CPC and that's link clicks and so these are basically the most important metrics that you're going to have now we want to kind of organize this so that it flows the way our funnel flows so that we can actually view our reporting column the way our funnel would flow so this is all pretty good here but we want to move soft stats above so we're going to put our we've got our click-through rate here we'll move unique link clicks we're going to use our cost per click below that we've got our ad to cart here that needs to go before purchases because people add to cart before they can buy we've got our positive feedback which we're going to put underneath oops our frequency because that's quite a soft start here put that there we've got our view content before at cart so that's fine we've got our registrations before our view content which is also the flow of our funnel and then you'll see here CPC lending page views etc so let's just move that here there we go okay now what we do is we can save this as a preset so we're going to go sales for example I think I already have one called that so let's just do this for now and now every time you want to see this you can just access it by clicking here you can also set this as the default so that every time you log in this is the dashboard that you're going to see and you can see here it's basically taking you through each step of your funnel amount spent click-through rate what's the click-through rate how many link clicks did you send what's the cost per link click again these are the stats that you're assessing here right CPC $1 CPC completed registration or whatever event you're tracking here content views website ad to cart and then purchases and so you can basically come in here and start calculating all the important metrics that you're trying to track for along the funnel and quickly assess where the bottlenecks are
